question_title: What will US airline passenger volume be for these weeks in April 2026, according to the TSA? (Apr 27–May 3, 2026)
summary:
Forecast for US TSA passenger volume Apr 27-May 3, 2026 (Mon-Sun). The median estimate of ~17.25M represents approximately a 4% decline from the 2025 late April comparable week (17.95M), roughly flat with the 2024/2025 average late-April week (~17.56M). 

Key driver: The jet fuel crisis from the Iran war is forcing US airlines to cut capacity (United -5%, Delta -3.5%) and raise fares (12-18% YoY), but early April 2026 TSA data shows remarkable resilience (+1.69% vs 2025 DOW-matched). The distribution reflects genuine uncertainty about crisis trajectory: the downside tail accounts for potential fuel shortages and further capacity cuts, while the upside reflects late April seasonal strength and US domestic market resilience. The estimate is consistent with my prior forecasts (Apr 6-12: ~17.92M partially observed, Apr 13-19: ~17.10M projected), with the late April seasonal uplift partially offsetting additional crisis deepening.

factors.description: Early April 2026 TSA data shows +1.69% vs 2025 DOW-matched, indicating resilient passenger volumes despite crisis
United Airlines cutting 5% capacity Q2-Q3, Delta cutting 3.5%, other carriers following with schedule reductions
Jet fuel prices doubled ($742->$1710/metric tonne), fares up 12-18% YoY, demand dampening expected
Late April seasonal uplift: 2025 showed +4% from mid-April to late April Mon-Sun weeks
US domestic refining capacity provides buffer vs Europe/Asia; North American impact less severe than global
Ryanair CEO warned of 5-10% summer flight cuts if conflict continues into May; crisis may deepen
factors.conditional: If crisis deepens significantly by late April

factors.description: 14.6% N.Am cancellation rate on one day (vs 4.4% prior year) shows volatility in disruptions
Historical baseline: 2024+2025 average late April Mon-Sun week is 17.56M

reasoning:
**Forecast: TSA Passenger Volume Apr 27–May 3, 2026**

**Median: ~17.25M passengers** | 80% CI: 16.06M – 18.11M

Key findings:

- **Historical baseline**: Late April Mon-Sun weeks averaged 17.56M across 2024-2025
- **2025 comparable**: Apr 21-27, 2025 (Mon-Sun) was 17.95M — the highest April week
- **Early April 2026 resilience**: DOW-matched TSA data shows +1.69% vs 2025, surprising given the crisis
- **Crisis headwinds**: United cutting 5% capacity, Delta cutting 3.5%, jet fuel prices doubled, fares up 12-18% YoY
- **Late April seasonal uplift** partially offsets additional crisis deepening vs my Apr 13-19 forecast (17.1M)

The distribution is left-skewed reflecting downside risk from potential crisis escalation, with 10th percentile at ~16.1M (significant disruption scenario) and 90th at ~18.1M (crisis stabilizes, seasonal strength prevails).

resolution_criteria:
This question is a subquestion of a group question. This subquestion specifically pertains to (and resolves according to) the option 'Apr 27–May 3, 2026'. The resolution criteria for the parent question is below. 

These subquestions resolve as the total volume of passengers screened by the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) at its checkpoints for the rolling 7-day periods in the listed options.

TSA posts the data at the following link:
[https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes](https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes)
fine_print: This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43112) which opened on 2026-04-06 23:04:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
